9 | # Design: Pure YAML + dataclass. No database dependency. User-editable.

11 | import logging
12 | import os
13 | from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
14 | from pathlib import Path
15 | from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
16 | 
17 | import yaml
18 | 
19 | logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
20 | 
21 | 
22 | @dataclass
23 | class Entity:
24 |     """A user-definable entity — a Pillar Keeper or custom persona."""
25 | 
26 |     name: str
27 |     domains: List[str]
28 |     model: str
29 |     personality: str
30 |     temperature: float = 0.7
31 |     context_window: int = 8192
32 |     pillar: Optional[str] = None
33 |     pantheon: Optional[str] = None
34 |     element: Optional[str] = None
35 |     chakra: Optional[str] = None
36 |     planet: Optional[str] = None
37 |     sigil: Optional[str] = None
38 |     glyph: Optional[str] = None
39 |     invocation: Optional[str] = None
40 |     role: Optional[str] = None
41 |     container: bool = False
42 |     port: Optional[int] = None
43 | 
44 |     def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
45 |         return {k: v for k, v in asdict(self).items() if v is not None}
46 | 
47 | 
48 | class EntityRegistry:
49 |     """Loads, saves, and manages entities from YAML config."""
50 | 
51 |     def __init__(self, config_path: Optional[str] = None):
52 |         if config_path is None:
53 |             config_path = os.environ.get(
54 |                 "OMEGA_ENTITIES_CONFIG",
55 |                 str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent / "config" / "entities.yaml"),
56 |             )
57 |         self.config_path = Path(config_path)
58 |         self._entities: Dict[str, Entity] = {}
59 |         self._load()
60 | 
61 |     def _load(self) -> None:
62 |         """Load entities from YAML file."""
63 |         if not self.config_path.exists():
64 |             logger.warning(f"Entity config not found at {self.config_path}")
65 |             self._entities = {}
66 |             return
67 | 
68 |         with open(self.config_path, "r") as f:
69 |             data = yaml.safe_load(f)
70 | 
71 |         raw_entities = data.get("entities", {}) if data else {}
72 |         for key, raw in raw_entities.items():
73 |             entity = Entity(
74 |                 name=raw.get("name", key),
75 |                 domains=raw.get("domains", []),
76 |                 model=raw.get("model", "qwen3-1.7b-q6_k"),
77 |                 personality=raw.get("personality", ""),
78 |                 temperature=raw.get("temperature", 0.7),
79 |                 context_window=raw.get("context_window", 8192),
80 |                 pillar=raw.get("pillar"),
81 |                 pantheon=raw.get("pantheon"),
82 |                 element=raw.get("element"),
83 |                 chakra=raw.get("chakra"),
84 |                 planet=raw.get("planet"),
85 |                 sigil=raw.get("sigil"),
86 |                 glyph=raw.get("glyph"),
87 |                 invocation=raw.get("invocation"),
88 |                 role=raw.get("role"),
89 |                 container=raw.get("container", False),
90 |                 port=raw.get("port"),
91 |             )
92 |             self._entities[entity.name.lower()] = entity
93 | 
94 |         # Also load Iris from root if present
95 |         iris_raw = data.get("iris") if data else None
96 |         if iris_raw:
97 |             iris = Entity(
98 |                 name=iris_raw.get("name", "Iris"),
99 |                 domains=iris_raw.get("domains", []),
100 |                 model=iris_raw.get("model", "functiongemma-270m-it-q6_k"),
101 |                 personality=iris_raw.get("personality", ""),
102 |                 temperature=iris_raw.get("temperature", 0.6),
103 |                 context_window=iris_raw.get("context_window", 4096),
104 |                 role=iris_raw.get("role", "Messenger — Always-On Interface"),
105 |                 container=iris_raw.get("container", True),
106 |                 port=iris_raw.get("port", 8080),
107 |             )
108 |             self._entities["iris"] = iris
109 | 
110 |         logger.info(f"Loaded {len(self._entities)} entities from config")
111 | 
112 |     def get(self, name: str) -> Optional[Entity]:
113 |         """Get entity by name (case-insensitive)."""
114 |         return self._entities.get(name.lower())
115 | 
116 |     def list(self) -> List[Entity]:
117 |         """List all entities (Pillar Keepers first, then Iris)."""
118 |         entities = [e for k, e in self._entities.items() if k != "iris"]
119 |         if "iris" in self._entities:
120 |             entities.append(self._entities["iris"])
121 |         return entities
122 | 
123 |     def list_pillar_keepers(self) -> List[Entity]:
124 |         """List only the 10 Pillar Keepers (entities with pillar fields)."""
125 |         return [e for e in self._entities.values() if e.pillar is not None]
126 | 
127 |     def names(self) -> List[str]:
128 |         """Return list of entity names."""
129 |         return [e.name for e in self.list()]
130 | 
131 |     def add(self, entity: Entity) -> None:
132 |         """Add a new entity. Overwrites if name exists."""
133 |         self._entities[entity.name.lower()] = entity
134 |         self._save()
135 | 
136 |     def remove(self, name: str) -> bool:
137 |         """Remove an entity by name. Returns True if removed."""
138 |         key = name.lower()
139 |         if key in self._entities:
140 |             del self._entities[key]
141 |             self._save()
142 |             return True
143 |         return False
144 | 
145 |     def find_by_domain(self, text: str) -> Optional[Entity]:
146 |         """Find the best entity match for a query text based on domain keywords.
147 | 
148 |         Matches Pillar Keepers only (Iris handles routing separately).
149 |         Scores each entity by how many domain keywords appear in the text.
150 |         Returns the highest-scoring entity, or None if no match.
151 |         """
152 |         text_lower = text.lower()
153 |         best_score = 0
154 |         best_entity: Optional[Entity] = None
155 | 
156 |         for entity in self.list_pillar_keepers():
157 |             score = 0
158 |             for keyword in entity.domains:
159 |                 if keyword in text_lower:
160 |                     score += 1
161 |             if score > best_score:
162 |                 best_score = score
163 |                 best_entity = entity
164 | 
165 |         return best_entity if best_score > 0 else None
166 | 
167 |     def find_by_name_fragment(self, fragment: str) -> Optional[Entity]:
168 |         """Find entity by partial name match (for 'summon' detection)."""
169 |         frag = fragment.lower()
170 |         for key, entity in self._entities.items():
171 |             if frag in key or frag in entity.name.lower():
172 |                 return entity
173 |         return None
174 | 
175 |     def _save(self) -> None:
176 |         """Save current entities back to YAML file.
177 | 
178 |         Note: This preserves the structure but may reformat comments.
179 |         Consider manual edits to entities.yaml for production changes.
180 |         """
181 |         data = {"entities": {}}
182 |         for key, entity in self._entities.items():
183 |             if key == "iris":
184 |                 continue
185 |             data["entities"][key] = entity.to_dict()
186 | 
187 |         if "iris" in self._entities:
188 |             data["iris"] = self._entities["iris"].to_dict()
189 | 
190 |         with open(self.config_path, "w") as f:
191 |             yaml.dump(data, f, default_flow_style=False, sort_keys=False, allow_unicode=True)
192 |         logger.info(f"Saved {len(self._entities)} entities to {self.config_path}")
193 | 
194 |     def count(self) -> int:
195 |         return len(self._entities)

2 | # AP: AP-OBSERVABILITY-v1.0.0

4 | #
5 | # Logs every query-response cycle with full provenance for:
6 | #   - Real-time monitoring (console + Redis streams)
7 | #   - Debugging and audit (file rotation)
8 | #   - Fine-tuning dataset generation (JSONL export)
9 | #
10 | # Every interaction gets a trace_id that follows it through
11 | # the entire Oracle → Entity → ModelGateway → Response pipeline.
12 | 
13 | import json
14 | import logging
15 | import os
16 | import time
17 | import uuid
18 | from datetime import datetime, timezone
19 | from pathlib import Path
20 | from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
21 | 
22 | import anyio
23 | 
24 | logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
25 | 
26 | # ── Storage paths ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
27 | DATA_DIR = Path(os.environ.get("OMEGA_DATA_DIR", str(Path.home() / "omega" / "data")))
28 | LOG_DIR = DATA_DIR / "logs"
29 | DATASET_DIR = DATA_DIR / "datasets"
30 | TRACE_DIR = DATA_DIR / "traces"
31 | 
32 | for d in [LOG_DIR, DATASET_DIR, TRACE_DIR]:
33 |     d.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
34 | 
35 | 
36 | # ── Trace ID ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
37 | def new_trace_id() -> str:
38 |     """Generate a unique trace ID for an interaction cycle."""
39 |     return f"trc_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
40 | 
41 | 
42 | # ── Event types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43 | class EventType:
44 |     QUERY_RECEIVED = "query.received"
45 |     SUMMON_DETECTED = "summon.detected"
46 |     DOMAIN_ROUTED = "domain.routed"
47 |     ENTITY_MATCHED = "entity.matched"
48 |     MODEL_INVOKED = "model.invoked"
49 |     MODEL_COMPLETED = "model.completed"
50 |     BACKEND_FALLBACK = "backend.fallback"
51 |     RESPONSE_DELIVERED = "response.delivered"
52 |     ESCALATION = "escalation"
53 |     IRIS_SPECULATIVE = "iris.speculative"
54 |     ERROR = "error"
55 | 
56 | 
57 | # ── Observability Engine ──────────────────────────────────────────────
58 | class ObservabilityEngine:
59 |     """Central observability, logging, and dataset collection."""
60 | 
61 |     def __init__(self, enable_dataset_collection: bool = True):
62 |         self.enable_dataset_collection = enable_dataset_collection
63 |         self._session_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]
64 |         self._event_log: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
65 |         self._dataset: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
66 | 
67 |     # ── Trace an entire interaction cycle ────────────────────────────
68 |     def trace(self, trace_id: Optional[str] = None) -> "TraceSession":
69 |         """Start a new trace session for an interaction."""
70 |         return TraceSession(
71 |             trace_id=trace_id or new_trace_id(),
72 |             engine=self,
73 |         )
74 | 
75 |     # ── Log an event ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
76 |     def log_event(
77 |         self,
78 |         event_type: str,
79 |         trace_id: str,
80 |         data: Dict[str, Any],
81 |     ) -> None:
82 |         """Log a single observability event."""
83 |         event = {
84 |             "event": event_type,
85 |             "trace_id": trace_id,
86 |             "session_id": self._session_id,
87 |             "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
88 |             "data": data,
89 |         }
90 |         self._event_log.append(event)
91 | 
92 |         # Also log to standard logger
93 |         logger.debug(f"[{trace_id}] {event_type}: {json.dumps(data, default=str)[:200]}")
94 | 
95 |     # ── Record a training example for fine-tuning ────────────────────
96 |     def record_training_example(
97 |         self,
98 |         trace_id: str,
99 |         query: str,
100 |         system_prompt: str,
101 |         response: str,
102 |         entity: str,
103 |         model: str,
104 |         backend: str,
105 |         confidence: float,
106 |         latency_ms: float,
107 |         rating: Optional[int] = None,
108 |     ) -> None:
109 |         """Save a query-response pair as a fine-tuning dataset entry."""
110 |         if not self.enable_dataset_collection:
111 |             return
112 | 
113 |         example = {
114 |             "trace_id": trace_id,
115 |             "session_id": self._session_id,
116 |             "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
117 |             "messages": [
118 |                 {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
119 |                 {"role": "user", "content": query},
120 |                 {"role": "assistant", "content": response},
121 |             ],
122 |             "metadata": {
123 |                 "entity": entity,
124 |                 "model": model,
125 |                 "backend": backend,
126 |                 "confidence": confidence,
127 |                 "latency_ms": latency_ms,
128 |                 "rating": rating,
129 |             },
130 |         }
131 |         self._dataset.append(example)
132 | 
133 |     # ── Persist dataset to disk ──────────────────────────────────────
134 |     async def flush_dataset(self) -> Optional[Path]:
135 |         """Write collected training examples to disk as JSONL."""
136 |         if not self._dataset:
137 |             return None
138 | 
139 |         timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
140 |         path = DATASET_DIR / f"finetune_{timestamp}.jsonl"
141 | 
142 |         async with await anyio.open_file(path, mode="a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
143 |             for example in self._dataset:
144 |                 await f.write(json.dumps(example, default=str) + "\n")
145 | 
146 |         count = len(self._dataset)
147 |         self._dataset = []
148 |         logger.info(f"Flushed {count} training examples to {path}")
149 |         return path
150 | 
151 |     # ── Get recent events for monitoring ─────────────────────────────
152 |     def recent_events(self, limit: int = 50) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
153 |         return self._event_log[-limit:]
154 | 
155 |     # ── Stats ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
156 |     def stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
157 |         event_counts: Dict[str, int] = {}
158 |         for event in self._event_log:
159 |             event_counts[event["event"]] = event_counts.get(event["event"], 0) + 1
160 |         return {
161 |             "total_events": len(self._event_log),
162 |             "dataset_size": len(self._dataset),
163 |             "event_counts": event_counts,
164 |             "session_id": self._session_id,
165 |         }
166 | 
167 | 
168 | class TraceSession:
169 |     """A single interaction trace. Context manager for easy lifecycle."""
170 | 
171 |     def __init__(self, trace_id: str, engine: ObservabilityEngine):
172 |         self.trace_id = trace_id
173 |         self.engine = engine
174 |         self.start_time: float = 0.0
175 |         self.data: Dict[str, Any] = {}
176 | 
177 |     async def __aenter__(self) -> "TraceSession":
178 |         self.start_time = time.monotonic()
179 |         return self
180 | 
181 |     async def __aexit__(self, *args) -> None:
182 |         self.data["total_latency_ms"] = (time.monotonic() - self.start_time) * 1000
183 |         # Auto-flush dataset periodically (every 100 interactions)
184 |         if len(self.engine._dataset) >= 100:
185 |             await self.engine.flush_dataset()
186 | 
187 |     def log(self, event_type: str, **data) -> None:
188 |         """Log an event within this trace."""
189 |         self.engine.log_event(event_type, self.trace_id, data)
190 | 
191 |     def record(
192 |         self,
193 |         query: str,
194 |         system_prompt: str,
195 |         response: str,
196 |         entity: str,
197 |         model: str,
198 |         backend: str,
199 |         confidence: float,
200 |         rating: Optional[int] = None,
201 |     ) -> None:
202 |         """Record a training example from this trace."""
203 |         latency_ms = (time.monotonic() - self.start_time) * 1000
204 |         self.engine.record_training_example(
205 |             trace_id=self.trace_id,
206 |             query=query,
207 |             system_prompt=system_prompt,
208 |             response=response,
209 |             entity=entity,
210 |             model=model,
211 |             backend=backend,
212 |             confidence=confidence,
213 |             latency_ms=latency_ms,
214 |             rating=rating,
215 |         )
216 | 
217 | 
218 | # ── Module-level singleton ────────────────────────────────────────────
219 | _engine: Optional[ObservabilityEngine] = None
220 | 
221 | 
222 | def get_engine() -> ObservabilityEngine:
223 |     global _engine
224 |     if _engine is None:
225 |         _engine = ObservabilityEngine()
226 |     return _engine

1 | # 🔱 Omega Oracle — Single Intelligence Facade
2 | # AP: AP-ORACLE-v2.0.0
3 | # ICS: [NODE: ARCHON | ARCHETYPE: SOPHIA | CONTEXT: ORACLE-ROUTER]
4 | #
5 | # The Oracle is the single entry point for user interaction.
6 | # It implements speculative decoding: Iris (lightweight) attempts first,
7 | # then escalates to domain-matched Pillar Keeper on low confidence.
8 | # Every interaction is traced, logged, and optionally saved for fine-tuning.
9 | #
10 | # Speculative decoder flow:
11 | #   Query → Iris (functiongemma-270m) → confidence check
12 | #     → high confidence → respond directly
13 | #     → low confidence → escalate to Pillar Keeper → model gateway → respond
14 | 
15 | import logging
16 | import re
17 | import time
18 | from dataclasses import dataclass, field
19 | from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
20 | 
21 | from ..observability import TraceSession, get_engine, new_trace_id
22 | from .entity_registry import Entity, EntityRegistry
23 | from .model_gateway import ModelGateway
24 | 
25 | logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
26 | 
27 | # Confidence threshold for Iris direct response vs escalation
28 | IRIS_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.4
29 | 
30 | 
31 | @dataclass
32 | class OracleResponse:
33 |     """Unified response from the Oracle."""
34 | 
35 |     text: str
36 |     entity: str
37 |     pillar: Optional[str] = None
38 |     role: Optional[str] = None
39 |     sigil: Optional[str] = None
40 |     glyph: Optional[str] = None
41 |     pantheon: Optional[str] = None
42 |     domains: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
43 |     confidence: float = 0.0
44 |     trace_id: Optional[str] = None
45 |     backend: Optional[str] = None
46 |     escalated: bool = False
47 | 
48 | 
49 | class Oracle:
50 |     """Single intelligence facade with speculative decoder pattern."""
51 | 
52 |     # Simple keywords that Iris can handle directly (low complexity)
53 |     IRIS_DIRECT_KEYWORDS = {
54 |         "hello", "hi", "hey", "greetings", "thanks", "thank you",
55 |         "bye", "goodbye", "help", "what can you do", "commands",
56 |     }
57 | 
58 |     # Query complexity indicators (trigger escalation)
59 |     COMPLEXITY_INDICATORS = {
60 |         "why", "how", "explain", "analyze", "compare", "contrast",
61 |         "difference between", "relationship between", "meaning of",
62 |         "define", "describe", "what is the", "tell me about",
63 |     }
64 | 
65 |     def __init__(
66 |         self,
67 |         registry: Optional[EntityRegistry] = None,
68 |         model_gateway: Optional[ModelGateway] = None,
69 |     ):
70 |         self.registry = registry or EntityRegistry()
71 |         self.model_gateway = model_gateway or ModelGateway()
72 |         self.default_entity = self.registry.get("brigid") or self.registry.get("sophia")
73 |         self.iris_entity = self.registry.get("iris")
74 |         self.observability = get_engine()
75 | 
76 |         # Compiled patterns
77 |         self.at_summon = re.compile(r"^@(\w+)[,:\s]+(.+)$", re.IGNORECASE)
78 |         self.hey_summon = re.compile(r"^(?:hey\s+|summon\s+)(.+?)[,:]\s*(.*)$", re.IGNORECASE)
79 |         self.consult_pattern = re.compile(r"^ask\s+(.+?)\s+about\s+(.+)$", re.IGNORECASE)
80 | 
81 |     # ── PUBLIC API ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
82 | 
83 |     async def talk(self, query: str) -> OracleResponse:
84 |         """Route a query through the speculative decoder.
85 | 
86 |         Speculative flow:
87 |           1. Iris (functiongemma-270m) attempts a lightweight response
88 |           2. If confidence > threshold OR query is simple → respond as Iris
89 |           3. Otherwise → escalate to domain-matched Pillar Keeper
90 |         """
91 |         async with self.observability.trace() as trace:
92 |             trace.log("query.received", query=query)
93 | 
94 |             if not query or not query.strip():
95 |                 return self._empty_response(trace)
96 | 
97 |             # Step 1: Try explicit summon (bypasses speculative decoder)
98 |             summoned = self._detect_summon(query)
99 |             if summoned:
100 |                 trace.log("summon.detected", entity=summoned[0], query=summoned[1])
101 |                 return await self._summon(summoned[0], summoned[1], trace)
102 | 
103 |             consulted = self._detect_consult(query)
104 |             if consulted:
105 |                 trace.log("summon.detected", entity=consulted[0], query=consulted[1], pattern="consult")
106 |                 return await self._summon(consulted[0], consulted[1], trace)
107 | 
108 |             # Step 2: Speculative decode — Iris tries first
109 |             iris_confidence = self._assess_iris_confidence(query)
110 |             trace.log("iris.speculative", confidence=iris_confidence, query=query)
111 | 
112 |             if iris_confidence > IRIS_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD:
113 |                 return await self._respond_as_iris(query, trace, iris_confidence)
114 | 
115 |             # Step 3: Escalate to domain-matched Pillar Keeper
116 |             trace.log("escalation", reason=f"iris_confidence={iris_confidence:.2f} <= threshold={IRIS_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD}")
117 |             return await self._route_by_domain(query, trace)
118 | 
119 |     async def summon(self, entity_name: str, query: str) -> OracleResponse:
120 |         """Directly summon a specific entity by name."""
121 |         async with self.observability.trace() as trace:
122 |             trace.log("summon.detected", entity=entity_name, query=query)
123 |             return await self._summon(entity_name, query, trace)
124 | 
125 |     # ── INTERNAL: Speculative decoder ─────────────────────────────────
126 | 
127 |     def _assess_iris_confidence(self, query: str) -> float:
128 |         """Assess whether Iris can handle this query directly.
129 | 
130 |         Returns confidence 0.0–1.0:
131 |           - 0.8+ for simple greetings/farewells
132 |           - 0.5 for help commands
133 |           - 0.3+ for short queries (Iris tries with small model)
134 |           - 0.0 for complex queries (escalate immediately)
135 |         """
136 |         query_lower = query.lower().strip()
137 | 
138 |         # Exact simple matches → high confidence
139 |         for keyword in self.IRIS_DIRECT_KEYWORDS:
140 |             if query_lower == keyword or query_lower.startswith(keyword):
141 |                 return 0.9
142 | 
143 |         # Short queries → moderate confidence (Iris can attempt)
144 |         if len(query_lower.split()) <= 3:
145 |             return 0.5
146 | 
147 |         # Complex indicators → low confidence (escalate)
148 |         for indicator in self.COMPLEXITY_INDICATORS:
149 |             if indicator in query_lower:
150 |                 return 0.0
151 | 
152 |         # Default: let Iris try with a small model, expect escalation
153 |         return 0.3
154 | 
155 |     async def _respond_as_iris(self, query: str, trace: TraceSession, confidence: float) -> OracleResponse:
156 |         """Respond directly as Iris with lightweight inference."""
157 |         trace.log("iris.speculative", action="direct_response", confidence=confidence)
158 | 
159 |         # If Iris entity exists, use her personality
160 |         system_prompt = "You are Iris, the Rainbow Bridge. Answer simply and warmly."
161 |         if self.iris_entity:
162 |             system_prompt = self.iris_entity.personality
163 | 
164 |         # Attempt inference with the lightest available model
165 |         response_text = await self.model_gateway.generate(
166 |             model_name="functiongemma-270m-it-q6_k",
167 |             system_prompt=system_prompt,
168 |             user_query=query,
169 |             temperature=0.6,
170 |             max_tokens=256,
171 |         )
172 | 
173 |         backend = await self.model_gateway.get_preferred_backend()
174 | 
175 |         result = OracleResponse(
176 |             text=response_text,
177 |             entity="Iris",
178 |             role="Messenger — Lightweight Response",
179 |             confidence=confidence,
180 |             trace_id=trace.trace_id,
181 |             backend=backend,
182 |             escalated=False,
183 |         )
184 | 
185 |         trace.log("response.delivered", entity="Iris", confidence=confidence, backend=backend)
186 |         return result
187 | 
188 |     async def _summon(self, entity_name: str, query: str, trace: TraceSession) -> OracleResponse:
189 |         """Summon a specific entity and generate a response."""
190 |         entity = self.registry.get(entity_name)
191 |         if not entity:
192 |             entity = self.registry.find_by_name_fragment(entity_name)
193 | 
194 |         if not entity:
195 |             trace.log("entity.not_found", entity=entity_name)
196 |             return await self._route_by_domain(query or entity_name, trace)
197 | 
198 |         trace.log("entity.matched", entity=entity.name, pillar=entity.pillar)
199 | 
200 |         # Generate response via model gateway
201 |         response_text = await self.model_gateway.generate(
202 |             model_name=entity.model,
203 |             system_prompt=entity.personality,
204 |             user_query=query,
205 |             temperature=entity.temperature,
206 |             max_tokens=1024,
207 |         )
208 | 
209 |         backend = await self.model_gateway.get_preferred_backend()
210 |         sigil_str = f" {entity.sigil}" if entity.sigil else ""
211 | 
212 |         result = OracleResponse(
213 |             text=f"{entity.name} says: {response_text}{sigil_str}",
214 |             entity=entity.name,
215 |             pillar=entity.pillar,
216 |             sigil=entity.sigil,
217 |             glyph=entity.glyph,
218 |             pantheon=entity.pantheon,
219 |             domains=entity.domains,
220 |             confidence=1.0,
221 |             trace_id=trace.trace_id,
222 |             backend=backend,
223 |         )
224 | 
225 |         trace.log("model.completed", entity=entity.name, backend=backend)
226 |         trace.record(
227 |             query=query,
228 |             system_prompt=entity.personality,
229 |             response=response_text,
230 |             entity=entity.name,
231 |             model=entity.model,
232 |             backend=backend,
233 |             confidence=1.0,
234 |         )
235 |         return result
236 | 
237 |     async def _route_by_domain(self, text: str, trace: TraceSession) -> OracleResponse:
238 |         """Route query to entity by domain keyword matching."""
239 |         entity = self.registry.find_by_domain(text)
240 | 
241 |         if not entity:
242 |             entity = self.default_entity
243 |             confidence = 0.3
244 |         else:
245 |             confidence = 0.7
246 | 
247 |         trace.log("domain.routed", entity=entity.name if entity else None, confidence=confidence)
248 | 
249 |         if not entity:
250 |             return OracleResponse(
251 |                 text="The Oracle is here. Speak your question.",
252 |                 entity="Oracle",
253 |                 confidence=0.0,
254 |                 trace_id=trace.trace_id,
255 |             )
256 | 
257 |         # Generate response via model gateway
258 |         response_text = await self.model_gateway.generate(
259 |             model_name=entity.model,
260 |             system_prompt=entity.personality,
261 |             user_query=text,
262 |             temperature=entity.temperature,
263 |             max_tokens=1024,
264 |         )
265 | 
266 |         backend = await self.model_gateway.get_preferred_backend()
267 |         sigil_str = f" {entity.sigil}" if entity.sigil else ""
268 | 
269 |         result = OracleResponse(
270 |             text=f"{entity.name} says: {response_text}{sigil_str}",
271 |             entity=entity.name,
272 |             pillar=entity.pillar,
273 |             sigil=entity.sigil,
274 |             glyph=entity.glyph,
275 |             pantheon=entity.pantheon,
276 |             domains=entity.domains,
277 |             confidence=confidence,
278 |             trace_id=trace.trace_id,
279 |             backend=backend,
280 |             escalated=True,
281 |         )
282 | 
283 |         trace.log("model.completed", entity=entity.name, backend=backend, escalated=True)
284 |         trace.record(
285 |             query=text,
286 |             system_prompt=entity.personality,
287 |             response=response_text,
288 |             entity=entity.name,
289 |             model=entity.model,
290 |             backend=backend,
291 |             confidence=confidence,
292 |         )
293 |         return result
294 | 
295 |     # ── Pattern detection ─────────────────────────────────────────────
296 | 
297 |     def _detect_summon(self, text: str) -> Optional[Tuple[str, str]]:
298 |         match = self.at_summon.match(text.strip())
299 |         if match:
300 |             return (match.group(1).strip(), match.group(2).strip())
301 |         match = self.hey_summon.match(text.strip())
302 |         if match:
303 |             return (match.group(1).strip(), match.group(2).strip())
304 |         sole_match = re.match(r"^@(\w+)\s*$", text.strip(), re.IGNORECASE)
305 |         if sole_match:
306 |             return (sole_match.group(1), "")
307 |         return None
308 | 
309 |     def _detect_consult(self, text: str) -> Optional[Tuple[str, str]]:
310 |         match = self.consult_pattern.match(text.strip())
311 |         if match:
312 |             return (match.group(1).strip(), match.group(2).strip())
313 |         return None
314 | 
315 |     def _empty_response(self, trace: TraceSession) -> OracleResponse:
316 |         trace.log("response.delivered", type="empty")
317 |         return OracleResponse(
318 |             text="Speak, and I will listen.",
319 |             entity=self.default_entity.name if self.default_entity else "Oracle",
320 |             trace_id=trace.trace_id,
321 |         )
322 | 
323 |     def set_default_entity(self, name: str) -> bool:
324 |         entity = self.registry.get(name)
325 |         if entity:
326 |             self.default_entity = entity
327 |             return True
328 |         return False

1 | """Entity Memory Store — Hot/Warm/Cold persistent memory for entities.
2 | 
3 | AP: AP-MEMORY-STORE-v1.0.0
4 | ICS: [NODE: MNEMOSYNE | ARCHETYPE: SOPHIA | CONTEXT: MEMORY-STORE]
5 | 
6 | Three-tier storage:
7 |   Hot   — In-memory LRU dict (active sessions, fast access)
8 |   Warm  — JSON files on disk (recent conversations, per-entity per-session)
9 |   Cold  — Gzipped JSON archive (compacted/summarized older sessions)
10 | 
11 | Every entity gets persistent conversation memory. Memory is injected
12 | into Oracle context on summon, and persisted after each response.
13 | 
14 | Auto-compaction: conversations > MAX_HISTORY exchanges are summarized.
15 | """

17 | import gzip
18 | import json
19 | import logging
20 | import os
21 | import time
22 | from collections import OrderedDict
23 | from datetime import datetime, timezone
24 | from pathlib import Path
25 | from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

27 | import anyio
28 | 
29 | logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
30 | 
31 | DATA_DIR = Path(os.environ.get("OMEGA_DATA_DIR", str(Path.home() / "omega" / "data")))
32 | MEMORY_DIR = DATA_DIR / "memory"
33 | TRACE_DIR = MEMORY_DIR / "trace"
34 | ENTITY_DIR = MEMORY_DIR / "entities"
35 | ARCHIVE_DIR = MEMORY_DIR / "archive"
36 | 
37 | for d in [MEMORY_DIR, TRACE_DIR, ENTITY_DIR, ARCHIVE_DIR]:
38 |     d.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
39 | 
40 | MAX_HOT_SESSIONS = 50
41 | MAX_HISTORY = 20
42 | MAX_CONTEXT_EXCHANGES = 6
43 | ARCHIVE_AFTER_DAYS = 7
44 | 
45 | 
46 | class MemoryStore:
47 |     """Hot/Warm/Cold entity memory with LRU caching."""
48 | 
49 |     def __init__(self):
50 |         self._hot: Dict[str, OrderedDict] = {}
51 |         self._stats: Dict[str, int] = {"loads": 0, "saves": 0, "archives": 0}
52 | 
53 |     def _entity_path(self, entity_name: str, session_id: str) -> Path:
54 |         safe_name = entity_name.lower().replace(" ", "_")
55 |         return ENTITY_DIR / safe_name / f"{session_id}.json"
56 | 
57 |     def _archive_path(self, entity_name: str, session_id: str) -> Path:
58 |         safe_name = entity_name.lower().replace(" ", "_")
59 |         return ARCHIVE_DIR / safe_name / f"{session_id}.json.gz"
60 | 
61 |     def _trace_path(self, trace_id: str) -> Path:
62 |         return TRACE_DIR / f"{trace_id}.json"
63 | 
64 |     async def get_history(
65 |         self,
66 |         entity_name: str,
67 |         session_id: str,
68 |         limit: int = MAX_CONTEXT_EXCHANGES,
69 |     ) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
70 |         """Get recent conversation history for context injection.
71 | 
72 |         Tries hot → warm → cold in order. Returns last N exchanges.
73 |         """
74 |         cache_key = f"{entity_name.lower()}:{session_id}"
75 | 
76 |         if cache_key in self._hot:
77 |             self._stats["loads"] += 1
78 |             history = list(self._hot[cache_key].values())
79 |             self._hot[cache_key].move_to_end(cache_key)
80 |             return history[-limit:]
81 | 
82 |         warm_path = self._entity_path(entity_name, session_id)
83 |         if warm_path.exists():
84 |             self._stats["loads"] += 1
85 |             async with await anyio.open_file(str(warm_path)) as f:
86 |                 data = json.loads(await f.read())
87 |             exchanges = data.get("exchanges", [])
88 |             self._cache_hot(cache_key, exchanges)
89 |             return exchanges[-limit:]
90 | 
91 |         cold_path = self._archive_path(entity_name, session_id)
92 |         if cold_path.exists():
93 |             self._stats["loads"] += 1
94 |             async with await anyio.open_file(str(cold_path), "rb") as f:
95 |                 data = json.loads(gzip.decompress(await f.read()))
96 |             exchanges = data.get("exchanges", [])
97 |             return exchanges[-limit:]
98 | 
99 |         return []
100 | 
101 |     async def add_exchange(
102 |         self,
103 |         entity_name: str,
104 |         session_id: str,
105 |         user_message: str,
106 |         response: str,
107 |         metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
108 |     ) -> None:
109 |         """Record a user-assistant exchange in entity memory.
110 | 
111 |         Stores in hot cache, syncs to warm on every call.
112 |         Auto-compacts if history exceeds MAX_HISTORY.
113 |         """
114 |         cache_key = f"{entity_name.lower()}:{session_id}"
115 |         exchange = {
116 |             "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
117 |             "user": user_message,
118 |             "assistant": response,
119 |             "metadata": metadata or {},
120 |         }
121 | 
122 |         if cache_key not in self._hot:
123 |             existing = await self.get_history(entity_name, session_id, limit=MAX_HISTORY)
124 |             self._cache_hot(cache_key, existing)
125 | 
126 |         self._hot[cache_key][str(time.time())] = exchange
127 |         exchanges = list(self._hot[cache_key].values())
128 | 
129 |         if len(exchanges) > MAX_HISTORY:
130 |             exchanges = await self._compact(entity_name, session_id, exchanges)
131 | 
132 |         warm_path = self._entity_path(entity_name, session_id)
133 |         warm_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
134 |         data = {
135 |             "entity": entity_name,
136 |             "session_id": session_id,
137 |             "exchange_count": len(exchanges),
138 |             "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
139 |             "exchanges": exchanges,
140 |         }
141 |         async with await anyio.open_file(str(warm_path), "w") as f:
142 |             await f.write(json.dumps(data, indent=2, default=str))
143 |         self._stats["saves"] += 1
144 | 
145 |     def _cache_hot(self, cache_key: str, exchanges: List[Dict]) -> None:
146 |         if cache_key not in self._hot:
147 |             self._hot[cache_key] = OrderedDict()
148 |         for i, ex in enumerate(exchanges):
149 |             self._hot[cache_key][f"hist_{i}"] = ex
150 |         while len(self._hot) > MAX_HOT_SESSIONS:
151 |             self._hot.popitem(last=False)
152 | 
153 |     async def _compact(
154 |         self,
155 |         entity_name: str,
156 |         session_id: str,
157 |         exchanges: List[Dict[str, Any]],
158 |     ) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
159 |         """Compact long conversation: keep first + last N exchanges, summarize middle."""
160 |         logger.info(f"Compacting {entity_name}/{session_id}: {len(exchanges)} exchanges")
161 |         self._stats["archives"] += 1
162 | 
163 |         if len(exchanges) <= MAX_HISTORY:
164 |             return exchanges
165 | 
166 |         keep = MAX_HISTORY // 2
167 |         kept = exchanges[:keep] + exchanges[-keep:]
168 |         middle_count = len(exchanges) - (keep * 2)
169 |         kept.insert(keep, {
170 |             "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
171 |             "system": f"[{middle_count} exchanges compacted]",
172 |             "user": "[summarized]",
173 |             "assistant": f"[{middle_count} previous exchanges were compacted. Context preserved.]",
174 |         })
175 |         return kept
176 | 
177 |     async def get_summary(
178 |         self,
179 |         entity_name: str,
180 |         session_id: str,
181 |     ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
182 |         """Get a summary of the conversation for this entity/session."""
183 |         exchanges = await self.get_history(entity_name, session_id, limit=MAX_HISTORY)
184 |         return {
185 |             "entity": entity_name,
186 |             "session_id": session_id,
187 |             "exchange_count": len(exchanges),
188 |             "last_exchange": exchanges[-1] if exchanges else None,
189 |             "first_exchange": exchanges[0] if exchanges else None,
190 |         }
191 | 
192 |     async def archive_session(
193 |         self,
194 |         entity_name: str,
195 |         session_id: str,
196 |     ) -> bool:
197 |         """Move a warm session to cold storage (gzipped)."""
198 |         warm_path = self._entity_path(entity_name, session_id)
199 |         if not warm_path.exists():
200 |             return False
201 | 
202 |         async with await anyio.open_file(str(warm_path)) as f:
203 |             data = json.loads(await f.read())
204 | 
205 |         cold_path = self._archive_path(entity_name, session_id)
206 |         cold_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
207 |         async with await anyio.open_file(str(cold_path), "wb") as f:
208 |             await f.write(gzip.compress(json.dumps(data, default=str).encode()))
209 | 
210 |         warm_path.unlink()
211 |         cache_key = f"{entity_name.lower()}:{session_id}"
212 |         self._hot.pop(cache_key, None)
213 |         self._stats["archives"] += 1
214 |         logger.info(f"Archived {entity_name}/{session_id} to cold storage")
215 |         return True
216 | 
217 |     async def trace_exchange(
218 |         self,
219 |         trace_id: str,
220 |         entity_name: str,
221 |         session_id: str,
222 |         user_message: str,
223 |         response: str,
224 |         metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
225 |     ) -> None:
226 |         """Record a trace-oriented exchange (linked to observability trace)."""
227 |         trace_data = {
228 |             "trace_id": trace_id,
229 |             "entity": entity_name,
230 |             "session_id": session_id,
231 |             "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
232 |             "user_message": user_message,
233 |             "response": response,
234 |             "metadata": metadata or {},
235 |         }
236 |         trace_path = self._trace_path(trace_id)
237 |         async with await anyio.open_file(str(trace_path), "w") as f:
238 |             await f.write(json.dumps(trace_data, indent=2, default=str))
239 | 
240 |     async def list_sessions(
241 |         self,
242 |         entity_name: Optional[str] = None,
243 |         limit: int = 20,
244 |     ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
245 |         """List recent sessions, optionally filtered by entity."""
246 |         sessions = []
247 |         if entity_name:
248 |             search_dir = ENTITY_DIR / entity_name.lower().replace(" ", "_")
249 |             if search_dir.exists():
250 |                 for path in sorted(search_dir.glob("*.json"), reverse=True)[:limit]:
251 |                     sessions.append({
252 |                         "session_id": path.stem,
253 |                         "entity": entity_name,
254 |                         "path": str(path),
255 |                     })
256 |         else:
257 |             for ent_dir in sorted(ENTITY_DIR.iterdir()):
258 |                 if ent_dir.is_dir():
259 |                     for path in sorted(ent_dir.glob("*.json"), reverse=True)[:limit]:
260 |                         sessions.append({
261 |                             "session_id": path.stem,
262 |                             "entity": ent_dir.name,
263 |                             "path": str(path),
264 |                         })
265 |         return sessions
266 | 
267 |     def stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
268 |         """Get memory store statistics."""
269 |         return {
270 |             "hot_sessions": sum(len(v) for v in self._hot.values()),
271 |             "hot_cache_size": len(self._hot),
272 |             "loads": self._stats["loads"],
273 |             "saves": self._stats["saves"],
274 |             "archives": self._stats["archives"],
275 |         }
276 | 
277 |     async def close(self) -> None:
278 |         """Flush hot cache to warm storage."""
279 |         for cache_key in list(self._hot.keys()):
280 |             entity_name, session_id = cache_key.rsplit(":", 1)
281 |             exchanges = list(self._hot[cache_key].values())
282 |             if exchanges:
283 |                 warm_path = self._entity_path(entity_name, session_id)
284 |                 warm_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
285 |                 data = {
286 |                     "entity": entity_name,
287 |                     "session_id": session_id,
288 |                     "exchange_count": len(exchanges),
289 |                     "last_updated": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
290 |                     "exchanges": exchanges,
291 |                 }
292 |                 async with await anyio.open_file(str(warm_path), "w") as f:
293 |                     await f.write(json.dumps(data, indent=2, default=str))
294 |         logger.info("Memory store flushed and closed")
295 | 
296 |     async def archive_old_sessions(self, older_than_days: int = ARCHIVE_AFTER_DAYS) -> int:
297 |         """Auto-archive sessions older than N days."""
298 |         count = 0
299 |         now = time.time()
300 |         for ent_dir in ENTITY_DIR.iterdir():
301 |             if not ent_dir.is_dir():
302 |                 continue
303 |             for path in ent_dir.glob("*.json"):
304 |                 age_days = (now - path.stat().st_mtime) / 86400
305 |                 if age_days > older_than_days:
306 |                     entity_name = ent_dir.name
307 |                     session_id = path.stem
308 |                     if await self.archive_session(entity_name, session_id):
309 |                         count += 1
310 |         return count

## Task 1: Create `src/omega/oracle/context_builder.py`

### Design
A `ContextBuilder` class that:
1. Accepts an entity name (or session ID) on construction or as parameters
2. Calls `MemoryStore.get_history()` to fetch recent conversation exchanges
3. Formats exchanges into a structured string block that can be prepended to an LLM system prompt
4. Optionally fetches recent observability trace IDs from `ObservabilityEngine.recent_events()`
5. Returns a clean string like:

```
## Recent Memory Context
[2026-05-13T16:00:00] User: what is strength?
[2026-05-13T16:00:01] Sekhmet: Strength is grounded truth...
```
6. Has a `build_context(entity_name, session_id, limit=6)` async method returning the string
7. Has a static `prepend_to_prompt(context_block, system_prompt)` utility that concatenates context + prompt

### Implementation details
- Uses `get_memory_store()` singleton from `memory_store.py`
- Uses `get_engine()` singleton from `observability.py` for recent trace event snippets
- Follows existing patterns: `anyio` for async, no external dependencies beyond stdlib
- Default limit of 6 exchanges (matches `MAX_CONTEXT_EXCHANGES` in `memory_store.py`)
- Graceful fallback: returns empty string if no memory available

36 | class ModelGateway:
37 |     """Abstracts local model inference. Auto-detects available backends.
38 | 
39 |     Supports Zen 2 optimizations:
40 |       - KV cache quantization per-model
41 |       - Adaptive thread count
42 |       - ONNX Runtime fallback for compatible models

44 | 
45 |     # Default backend URLs

50 |     def __init__(self, config_path: Optional[str] = None):
51 |         if config_path is None:
52 |             config_path = os.environ.get(
53 |                 "OMEGA_MODELS_CONFIG",
54 |                 str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent / "config" / "models.yaml"),
55 |             )
56 |         self.config_path = Path(config_path)
57 |         self.models = self._load_models()

59 |         self._backend_cache: Dict[str, bool] = {}
60 |         self._cpu_optimizer: Optional[Any] = None
61 |         self.resource_guard = ResourceGuard()
62 |         self._mock_backend = OfflineMockBackend()

64 |     def _load_models(self) -> dict:
65 |         """Load model specs from config."""
66 |         if not self.config_path.exists():
67 |             logger.warning(f"Models config not found at {self.config_path}")
68 |             return {}
69 |         with open(self.config_path, "r") as f:
70 |             data = yaml.safe_load(f)
71 |         return data.get("models", {}) if data else {}

73 |     def _load_kv_cache_config(self) -> dict:
74 |         """Load KV cache quantization config."""
75 |         if not self.config_path.exists():
76 |             return {}
77 |         with open(self.config_path, "r") as f:
78 |             data = yaml.safe_load(f)
79 |         return data.get("kv_cache", {}) if data else {}

81 |     def get_kv_cache_flags(self, model_name: str) -> List[str]:
82 |         """Get llama-server KV cache quantization flags for a model.
83 | 
84 |         Uses per-model config if available, otherwise default.
85 |         """
86 |         default_key = self._kv_cache_config.get("default_key_type", "q8_0")
87 |         default_value = self._kv_cache_config.get("default_value_type", "q8_0")
88 |         per_model = self._kv_cache_config.get("models", {}).get(model_name, {})
89 | 
90 |         key_type = per_model.get("key_type", default_key)
91 |         value_type = per_model.get("value_type", default_value)
92 | 
93 |         return ["-ctk", key_type, "-ctv", value_type, "-mli", "1"]

95 |     def get_model_path(self, model_name: str) -> Optional[str]:
96 |         """Get the GGUF path for a model by name."""
97 |         spec = self.models.get(model_name)
98 |         if not spec:
99 |             return None
100 |         path = spec.get("path")
101 |         if path and Path(path).exists():
102 |             return path
103 |         for alt in spec.get("alt_paths", []):

105 |                 return alt

125 |         try:

129 |                 return r.status_code == 200

135 |         try:

139 |                 return r.status_code == 200

160 |         """Detect all available inference backends. Returns dict of name -> available."""
161 |         backends = {
162 |             "lm_studio": await self._check_lm_studio(),
163 |             "ollama": await self._check_ollama(),
164 |             "llama_cpp": await self._check_llama_cpp(),
165 |             "llama_cli": self._check_llama_cli(),

172 |         """Return the name of the best available backend."""

174 |         for name in ["ollama", "lm_studio", "llama_cpp", "llama_cli", "llmster"]:
175 |             if backends.get(name):

181 |         """Resolve config model name to an Ollama tag.

183 |         Checks available Ollama models and finds the best match.
184 |         Falls back to the first available model if no match found.
185 |         """
186 |         try:

193 |                     # Try exact match first
194 |                     if model_name in available:

197 |                     name_lower = model_name.lower().split("-")[0].split("_")[0]

235 |                         return response
236 |                 except Exception as e:

287 |     # ── Backend: Ollama (OpenAI-compatible API) ──────────────────────
288 |     async def _try_ollama(
289 |         self, model: str, system_prompt: str, user_query: str, temperature: float, max_tokens: int
290 |     ) -> Optional[str]:
291 |         """Inference via Ollama's OpenAI-compatible API at 127.0.0.1:11434."""
292 |         import httpx
293 | 
294 |         messages = [
295 |             {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
296 |             {"role": "user", "content": user_query},
297 |         ]
298 |         payload = {
299 |             "model": model,
300 |             "messages": messages,
301 |             "temperature": temperature,
302 |             "max_tokens": max_tokens,
303 |             "stream": False,
304 |         }
305 | 
306 |         async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120.0) as client:
307 |             response = await client.post(f"{self.OLLAMA_URL}/v1/chat/completions", json=payload)
308 |             response.raise_for_status()
309 |             data = response.json()
310 |             choices = data.get("choices", [])
311 |             if choices:
312 |                 return choices[0].get("message", {}).get("content", "").strip()
313 |         return None
314 | 
315 |     # ── Backend: LM Studio (OpenAI-compatible API) ────────────────────
316 |     async def _try_lm_studio(
317 |         self, model: str, system_prompt: str, user_query: str, temperature: float, max_tokens: int
318 |     ) -> Optional[str]:
319 |         """Inference via LM Studio's OpenAI-compatible API at 127.0.0.1:1234."""
320 |         import httpx
321 | 
322 |         messages = [
323 |             {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
324 |             {"role": "user", "content": user_query},
325 |         ]
326 |         payload = {
327 |             "model": model,
328 |             "messages": messages,
329 |             "temperature": temperature,
330 |             "max_tokens": max_tokens,
331 |             "stream": False,
332 |         }
333 | 
334 |         async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120.0) as client:
335 |             response = await client.post(f"{self.LM_STUDIO_URL}/v1/chat/completions", json=payload)
336 |             response.raise_for_status()
337 |             data = response.json()
338 |             choices = data.get("choices", [])
339 |             if choices:
340 |                 return choices[0].get("message", {}).get("content", "").strip()
341 |         return None
342 | 
343 |     # ── Backend: llama.cpp HTTP server ────────────────────────────────
344 |     async def _try_llama_server(
345 |         self, system_prompt: str, user_query: str, temperature: float, max_tokens: int
346 |     ) -> Optional[str]:
347 |         """Inference via llama.cpp HTTP server at 127.0.0.1:8080."""
348 |         import httpx
349 | 
350 |         prompt = f"{system_prompt}\n\nUser: {user_query}\n\nEntity:"
351 |         payload = {
352 |             "prompt": prompt,
353 |             "temperature": temperature,
354 |             "max_tokens": max_tokens,
355 |             "stop": ["User:", "\n\n"],
356 |         }
357 | 
358 |         async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120.0) as client:
359 |             response = await client.post(f"{self.LLAMA_CPP_URL}/completion", json=payload)
360 |             response.raise_for_status()
361 |             data = response.json()
362 |             return data.get("content", "").strip()
363 | 
364 |     # ── Backend: Direct GGUF CLI (llama-cli or llmster) ───────────────
365 |     async def _try_direct_gguf(
366 |         self, cli_name: str, model_path: str, system_prompt: str, user_query: str,
367 |         temperature: float, max_tokens: int
368 |     ) -> Optional[str]:
369 |         """Inference via direct CLI subprocess (llama-cli or llmster)."""
370 |         prompt = f"<|system|>{system_prompt}</s><|user|>{user_query}</s><|assistant|>"
371 | 
372 |         result = await anyio.run_process(
373 |             [
374 |                 cli_name,
375 |                 "--model", model_path,
376 |                 "--prompt", prompt,
377 |                 "--temp", str(temperature),
378 |                 "--n-predict", str(max_tokens),
379 |                 "--no-display-prompt",
380 |             ],
381 |             capture_output=True,
382 |             check=False,
383 |         )
384 | 
385 |         if result.returncode == 0:
386 |             output = result.stdout.decode().strip()
387 |             return output if output else None
388 |         return None
389 | 
390 |     # ── Backend: ONNX Runtime ─────────────────────────────────────────
391 |     async def _try_onnx(
392 |         self, model_path: str, system_prompt: str, user_query: str,
393 |         temperature: float, max_tokens: int
394 |     ) -> Optional[str]:
395 |         """Inference via ONNX Runtime (optimized for Zen 2 CPU)."""
396 |         try:
397 |             import onnxruntime as ort
398 |         except ImportError:
399 |             logger.debug("ONNX Runtime not installed; skipping ONNX backend")
400 |             return None
401 | 
402 |         try:
403 |             sess = ort.InferenceSession(
404 |                 model_path,
405 |                 providers=["CPUExecutionProvider"],
406 |                 sess_options=ort.SessionOptions(),
407 |             )
408 |             input_name = sess.get_inputs()[0].name
409 |             # Simple text completion via ONNX (for compatible models)
410 |             input_text = f"{system_prompt}\n\nUser: {user_query}\n\nAssistant:"
411 |             inputs = {input_name: [input_text]}
412 |             outputs = sess.run(None, inputs)
413 |             if outputs and len(outputs[0]) > 0:
414 |                 return str(outputs[0][0])[:max_tokens]
415 |         except Exception as e:
416 |             logger.debug(f"ONNX inference failed: {e}")
417 |         return None
418 | 
419 |     def is_onnx_available(self) -> bool:
420 |         """Check if ONNX Runtime is installed and usable."""
421 |         try:
422 |             import onnxruntime as ort
423 |             return "CPUExecutionProvider" in ort.get_available_providers()
424 |         except ImportError:
425 |             return False
426 | 
427 |     # ── Fallback ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
428 |     def _fallback_response(self, model_name: str, system_prompt: str, user_query: str) -> str:
429 |         """Return a graceful fallback with setup instructions."""
430 |         entity_name = "Oracle"
431 |         for name, spec in self.models.items():
432 |             if name == model_name:
433 |                 entity_name = spec.get("entity", "Oracle").split(",")[0].strip()
434 |                 break
435 | 
436 |         return (
437 |             f"{entity_name} is here, but no inference backend is running.\n\n"
438 |             f"Quick options:\n"
439 |             f"  1. `ollama run qwen3:1.7b` — simplest, auto-detected at :11434\n"
440 |             f"  2. Open LM Studio and load a model (auto-detected at :1234)\n"
441 |             f"  3. Run llama-server: `llama-server --model path/to/model.gguf`\n\n"
442 |             f"Then try: `omega summon {entity_name.title()} \"{user_query}\"`"
443 |         )
444 | 
445 |     # ── Diagnostics ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
446 |     async def check_health(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
447 |         """Return health status of all inference backends."""
448 |         backends = await self.detect_backends()
449 |         return {
450 |             "ollama": {
451 |                 "available": backends.get("ollama", False),
452 |                 "url": self.OLLAMA_URL,

454 |             },
455 |             "lm_studio": {

472 |             },

<final_file_content path="src/omega/oracle/model_gateway.py">
# 🔱 Model Gateway — Local Inference Abstraction
# AP: AP-MODEL-GATEWAY-v2.2.0
# ICS: [NODE: ARCHON | ARCHETYPE: HERMES | CONTEXT: MODEL-ABSTRACTION]
#
# Supports 6 inference backends with automatic detection and fallback:
#   1. LM Studio   (OpenAI-compatible API at http://127.0.0.1:1234)
#   2. Ollama      (OpenAI-compatible API at http://127.0.0.1:11434)
#   3. llama.cpp   (HTTP server at http://127.0.0.1:8080)
#   4. llama-cli   (Direct GGUF via subprocess)
#   5. llmster     (Direct GGUF via subprocess)
#   6. ONNX Runtime (if models available in ONNX format)
#   7. Graceful fallback (no model loaded)
#
# Zen 2 optimizations:
#   - KV cache quantization (q8_0 key, q8_0 value by default)
#   - Adaptive thread pool (6 threads, pinned to cores 0,2,4,6)
#   - Cache-friendly batch sizes (512/32 for small models, 64/16 for 8B+)
#   - OMP_PROC_BIND=close for NUMA-aware scheduling on single-CCX

import json
import logging
import os
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

import anyio
import yaml

from .backends.mock import OfflineMockBackend
from .resource_guard import ResourceGuard

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class ModelGateway:
    """Abstracts local model inference. Auto-detects available backends.

    Supports Zen 2 optimizations:
      - KV cache quantization per-model
      - Adaptive thread count
      - ONNX Runtime fallback for compatible models
    """

    # Default backend URLs
    LM_STUDIO_URL = "http://127.0.0.1:1234"
    OLLAMA_URL = "http://127.0.0.1:11434"
    LLAMA_CPP_URL = "http://127.0.0.1:8080"

    def __init__(self, config_path: Optional[str] = None):
        if config_path is None:
            config_path = os.environ.get(
                "OMEGA_MODELS_CONFIG",
                str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent / "config" / "models.yaml"),
            )
        self.config_path = Path(config_path)
        self.models = self._load_models()
        self._kv_cache_config = self._load_kv_cache_config()
        self._backend_cache: Dict[str, bool] = {}
        self._cpu_optimizer: Optional[Any] = None
        self.resource_guard = ResourceGuard()
        self._mock_backend = OfflineMockBackend()

    def _load_models(self) -> dict:
        """Load model specs from config."""
        if not self.config_path.exists():
            logger.warning(f"Models config not found at {self.config_path}")
            return {}
        with open(self.config_path, "r") as f:
            data = yaml.safe_load(f)
        return data.get("models", {}) if data else {}

    def _load_kv_cache_config(self) -> dict:
        """Load KV cache quantization config."""
        if not self.config_path.exists():
            return {}
        with open(self.config_path, "r") as f:
            data = yaml.safe_load(f)
        return data.get("kv_cache", {}) if data else {}

    def get_kv_cache_flags(self, model_name: str) -> List[str]:
        """Get llama-server KV cache quantization flags for a model.

        Uses per-model config if available, otherwise default.
        """
        default_key = self._kv_cache_config.get("default_key_type", "q8_0")
        default_value = self._kv_cache_config.get("default_value_type", "q8_0")
        per_model = self._kv_cache_config.get("models", {}).get(model_name, {})

        key_type = per_model.get("key_type", default_key)
        value_type = per_model.get("value_type", default_value)

        return ["-ctk", key_type, "-ctv", value_type, "-mli", "1"]

    def get_model_path(self, model_name: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """Get the GGUF path for a model by name."""
        spec = self.models.get(model_name)
        if not spec:
            return None
        path = spec.get("path")
        if path and Path(path).exists():
            return path
        for alt in spec.get("alt_paths", []):
            if Path(alt).exists():
                return alt
        return path

    def get_model_spec(self, model_name: str) -> Optional[dict]:
        """Get full model spec."""
        return self.models.get(model_name)

    # ── Backend availability detection ─────────────────────────────────
    async def _check_lm_studio(self) -> bool:
        """Check if LM Studio is running (127.0.0.1:1234/v1/models)."""
        try:
            import httpx
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=2.0) as client:
                r = await client.get(f"{self.LM_STUDIO_URL}/v1/models")
                return r.status_code == 200
        except Exception:
            return False

    async def _check_ollama(self) -> bool:
        """Check if Ollama is running (127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags)."""
        try:
            import httpx
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=2.0) as client:
                r = await client.get(f"{self.OLLAMA_URL}/api/tags")
                return r.status_code == 200
        except Exception:
            return False

    async def _check_llama_cpp(self) -> bool:
        """Check if llama.cpp server is running (127.0.0.1:8080/health)."""
        try:
            import httpx
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=2.0) as client:
                r = await client.get(f"{self.LLAMA_CPP_URL}/health")
                return r.status_code == 200
        except Exception:
            return False

    def _check_llama_cli(self) -> bool:
        """Check if llama-cli binary is available."""
        try:
            subprocess.run(["llama-cli", "--version"], capture_output=True, check=False)
            return True
        except FileNotFoundError:
            return False

    def _check_llmster(self) -> bool:
        """Check if llmster binary is available."""
        try:
            subprocess.run(["llmster", "--version"], capture_output=True, check=False)
            return True
        except FileNotFoundError:
            return False

    async def detect_backends(self) -> Dict[str, bool]:
        """Detect all available inference backends. Returns dict of name -> available."""
        backends = {
            "lm_studio": await self._check_lm_studio(),
            "ollama": await self._check_ollama(),
            "llama_cpp": await self._check_llama_cpp(),
            "llama_cli": self._check_llama_cli(),
            "llmster": self._check_llmster(),
        }
        self._backend_cache = backends
        return backends

    async def get_preferred_backend(self) -> str:
        """Return the name of the best available backend.
        
        Priority: LM Studio → Ollama → llama.cpp → llama-cli → llmster.
        LM Studio is preferred as the primary headless inference server.
        Ollama is retained as a lightweight fallback/backup option.
        """
        backends = await self.detect_backends()
        for name in ["lm_studio", "ollama", "llama_cpp", "llama_cli", "llmster"]:
            if backends.get(name):
                return name
        return "none"

    # ── Model name resolution ──────────────────────────────────────────
    async def _resolve_ollama_model(self, model_name: str) -> str:
        """Resolve config model name to an Ollama tag.

        Checks available Ollama models and finds the best match.
        Falls back to the first available model if no match found.
        """
        try:
            import httpx
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=3.0) as client:
                r = await client.get(f"{self.OLLAMA_URL}/api/tags")
                if r.status_code == 200:
                    data = r.json()
                    available = [m["name"] for m in data.get("models", [])]
                    # Try exact match first
                    if model_name in available:
                        return model_name
                    # Try prefix match (e.g. 'krikri' matches 'krikri:latest')
                    name_lower = model_name.lower().split("-")[0].split("_")[0]
                    for tag in available:
                        if tag.lower().startswith(name_lower):
                            return tag
                    # Return first available as fallback
                    if available:
                        return available[0]
        except Exception:
            pass
        return model_name

    # ── Generation (auto-detect + fallback chain) ──────────────────────
    async def generate(
        self,
        model_name: str,
        system_prompt: str,
        user_query: str,
        temperature: float = 0.7,
        max_tokens: int = 1024,
    ) -> str:
        """Send a prompt to the model, auto-detecting best available backend.

        Fallback chain: LM Studio → Ollama → llama.cpp → ONNX → llama-cli → llmster → graceful
        """
        if os.environ.get("OMEGA_ENV") == "test":
            return await self._mock_backend.generate(
                model_name, system_prompt, user_query, temperature, max_tokens
            )

        async with self.resource_guard.lock():
            model_path = self.get_model_path(model_name)

            # 1. Try LM Studio (primary — OpenAI-compatible API, zero config)
            if await self._check_lm_studio():
                try:
                    response = await self._try_lm_studio(model_name, system_prompt, user_query, temperature, max_tokens)
                    if response:
                        return response
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.warning(f"LM Studio failed: {e}")

            # 2. Try Ollama (backup — OpenAI-compatible API, best model mgmt)
            if await self._check_ollama():
                try:
                    ollama_model = await self._resolve_ollama_model(model_name)
                    response = await self._try_ollama(ollama_model, system_prompt, user_query, temperature, max_tokens)
                    if response:
                        return response
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.warning(f"Ollama failed: {e}")

            # 3. Try llama.cpp HTTP server
            if await self._check_llama_cpp():
                try:
                    response = await self._try_llama_server(system_prompt, user_query, temperature, max_tokens)
                    if response:
                        return response
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.warning(f"llama.cpp server failed: {e}")

            # 4. Try ONNX Runtime (for embedding models or converted GGUFs)
            if model_path and Path(model_path).exists():
                try:
                    response = await self._try_onnx(model_path, system_prompt, user_query, temperature, max_tokens)
                    if response:
                        return response
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.warning(f"ONNX Runtime failed: {e}")

            # 5. Try direct llama-cli subprocess
            if model_path and Path(model_path).exists() and self._check_llama_cli():
                try:
                    response = await self._try_direct_gguf("llama-cli", model_path, system_prompt, user_query, temperature, max_tokens)
                    if response:
                        return response
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.warning(f"llama-cli failed: {e}")

            # 6. Try llmster subprocess
            if model_path and Path(model_path).exists() and self._check_llmster():
                try:
                    response = await self._try_direct_gguf("llmster", model_path, system_prompt, user_query, temperature, max_tokens)
                    if response:
                        return response
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.warning(f"llmster failed: {e}")

            # 7. Graceful fallback
            return self._fallback_response(model_name, system_prompt, user_query)

    # ── Backend: Ollama (OpenAI-compatible API) ──────────────────────
    async def _try_ollama(
        self, model: str, system_prompt: str, user_query: str, temperature: float, max_tokens: int
    ) -> Optional[str]:
        """Inference via Ollama's OpenAI-compatible API at 127.0.0.1:11434."""
        import httpx

        messages = [
            {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
            {"role": "user", "content": user_query},
        ]
        payload = {
            "model": model,
            "messages": messages,
            "temperature": temperature,
            "max_tokens": max_tokens,
            "stream": False,
        }

        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120.0) as client:
            response = await client.post(f"{self.OLLAMA_URL}/v1/chat/completions", json=payload)
            response.raise_for_status()
            data = response.json()
            choices = data.get("choices", [])
            if choices:
                return choices[0].get("message", {}).get("content", "").strip()
        return None

    # ── Backend: LM Studio (OpenAI-compatible API) ────────────────────
    async def _try_lm_studio(
        self, model: str, system_prompt: str, user_query: str, temperature: float, max_tokens: int
    ) -> Optional[str]:
        """Inference via LM Studio's OpenAI-compatible API at 127.0.0.1:1234."""
        import httpx

        messages = [
            {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
            {"role": "user", "content": user_query},
        ]
        payload = {
            "model": model,
            "messages": messages,
            "temperature": temperature,
            "max_tokens": max_tokens,
            "stream": False,
        }

        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120.0) as client:
            response = await client.post(f"{self.LM_STUDIO_URL}/v1/chat/completions", json=payload)
            response.raise_for_status()
            data = response.json()
            choices = data.get("choices", [])
            if choices:
                return choices[0].get("message", {}).get("content", "").strip()
        return None

    # ── Backend: llama.cpp HTTP server ────────────────────────────────
    async def _try_llama_server(
        self, system_prompt: str, user_query: str, temperature: float, max_tokens: int
    ) -> Optional[str]:
        """Inference via llama.cpp HTTP server at 127.0.0.1:8080."""
        import httpx

        prompt = f"{system_prompt}\n\nUser: {user_query}\n\nEntity:"
        payload = {
            "prompt": prompt,
            "temperature": temperature,
            "max_tokens": max_tokens,
            "stop": ["User:", "\n\n"],
        }

        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=120.0) as client:
            response = await client.post(f"{self.LLAMA_CPP_URL}/completion", json=payload)
            response.raise_for_status()
            data = response.json()
            return data.get("content", "").strip()

    # ── Backend: Direct GGUF CLI (llama-cli or llmster) ───────────────
    async def _try_direct_gguf(
        self, cli_name: str, model_path: str, system_prompt: str, user_query: str,
        temperature: float, max_tokens: int
    ) -> Optional[str]:
        """Inference via direct CLI subprocess (llama-cli or llmster)."""
        prompt = f"<|system|>{system_prompt}</s><|user|>{user_query}</s><|assistant|>"

        result = await anyio.run_process(
            [
                cli_name,
                "--model", model_path,
                "--prompt", prompt,
                "--temp", str(temperature),
                "--n-predict", str(max_tokens),
                "--no-display-prompt",
            ],
            capture_output=True,
            check=False,
        )

        if result.returncode == 0:
            output = result.stdout.decode().strip()
            return output if output else None
        return None

    # ── Backend: ONNX Runtime ─────────────────────────────────────────
    async def _try_onnx(
        self, model_path: str, system_prompt: str, user_query: str,
        temperature: float, max_tokens: int
    ) -> Optional[str]:
        """Inference via ONNX Runtime (optimized for Zen 2 CPU)."""
        try:
            import onnxruntime as ort
        except ImportError:
            logger.debug("ONNX Runtime not installed; skipping ONNX backend")
            return None

        try:
            sess = ort.InferenceSession(
                model_path,
                providers=["CPUExecutionProvider"],
                sess_options=ort.SessionOptions(),
            )
            input_name = sess.get_inputs()[0].name
            # Simple text completion via ONNX (for compatible models)
            input_text = f"{system_prompt}\n\nUser: {user_query}\n\nAssistant:"
            inputs = {input_name: [input_text]}
            outputs = sess.run(None, inputs)
            if outputs and len(outputs[0]) > 0:
                return str(outputs[0][0])[:max_tokens]
        except Exception as e:
            logger.debug(f"ONNX inference failed: {e}")
        return None

    def is_onnx_available(self) -> bool:
        """Check if ONNX Runtime is installed and usable."""
        try:
            import onnxruntime as ort
            return "CPUExecutionProvider" in ort.get_available_providers()
        except ImportError:
            return False

    # ── Fallback ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    def _fallback_response(self, model_name: str, system_prompt: str, user_query: str) -> str:
        """Return a graceful fallback with setup instructions."""
        entity_name = "Oracle"
        for name, spec in self.models.items():
            if name == model_name:
                entity_name = spec.get("entity", "Oracle").split(",")[0].strip()
                break

        return (
            f"{entity_name} is here, but no inference backend is running.\n\n"
            f"Quick options:\n"
            f"  1. `ollama run qwen3:1.7b` — simplest, auto-detected at :11434\n"
            f"  2. Open LM Studio and load a model (auto-detected at :1234)\n"
            f"  3. Run llama-server: `llama-server --model path/to/model.gguf`\n\n"
            f"Then try: `omega summon {entity_name.title()} \"{user_query}\"`"
        )

    # ── Diagnostics ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
    async def check_health(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Return health status of all inference backends."""
        backends = await self.detect_backends()
        return {
            "ollama": {
                "available": backends.get("ollama", False),
                "url": self.OLLAMA_URL,
                "note": "Simplest option. Run: ollama run qwen3:1.7b"
            },
            "lm_studio": {
                "available": backends.get("lm_studio", False),
                "url": self.LM_STUDIO_URL,
                "note": "OpenAI-compatible. Load model in LM Studio GUI."
            },
            "llama_cpp": {
                "available": backends.get("llama_cpp", False),
                "url": self.LLAMA_CPP_URL,
                "note": "llama.cpp HTTP server. Run: llama-server ..."
            },
            "llama_cli": {
                "available": backends.get("llama_cli", False),
                "note": "Direct GGUF inference. Run: llama-cli --model ..."
            },
            "llmster": {
                "available": backends.get("llmster", False),
                "note": "Zero-config CLI inference."
            },
        }

    async def is_server_alive(self) -> bool:
        """Check if any inference backend is available."""
        backends = await self.detect_backends()
        return any(backends.values())
